vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-705: Command taxonomy reorganization: clean up pan's command surface

plan.title: Command taxonomy reorganization: clean up pan's command surface
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: pan's command surface grew organically: pan work is a junk drawer mixing lifecycle/runtime/review/state/hooks; plumbing (cloister, specialists, db, beads, remote) crowds the happy path; the Claude Code skill set drifted from CLI verbs with no umbrella /pan. New users can't guess commands; rename churn is constant.
narratives.Proposal: Adopt a five-bucket taxonomy (lifecycle verbs, observation, managed nouns, system/first-run, pan admin) per PRD. Clean break, no aliases. Dashboard routes rename in lockstep. Skill surface aligns 1:1 with CLI verbs; umbrella /pan skill dispatches subcommands via Bash. Lock the output with plain-text fixture tests. Decompose aggressively: one bead per collapsed command, renamed verb, moved admin group, renamed route, and skill-alignment substep.

narrative.Action: git add docs/prds/planned/pan-command-taxonomy-reorg.md and docs/QUICK-REFERENCE.md on the first commit of the feature branch. Link the quick-ref from docs/INDEX.md so it is discoverable from the top.

subItems.title: docs/prds/planned/pan-command-taxonomy-reorg.md is tracked in git
docs/QUICK-REFERENCE.md is tracked in git
docs/INDEX.md links to docs/QUICK-REFERENCE.md

narrative.Action: Create src/cli/lib/resolve-issue-id.ts exporting resolveIssueId(arg: string): string. Centralizes the parsing/normalization (e.g. 'pan-705' vs 'PAN-705') currently duplicated across work/* handlers. Every lifecycle verb rewritten in later beads imports from this helper.

subItems.title: Helper exported from src/cli/lib/resolve-issue-id.ts with unit tests
Helper handles lowercase, uppercase, and numeric-only forms
Errors with a clear message when the input cannot be resolved to a known tracker prefix

narrative.Action: New src/cli/commands/show.ts. Default view: compact summary (shadow state line, current specialist + last heartbeat, top-3 cv entries, health summary). Flags --cv / --context / --health / --shadow / --json scope the output. Handlers delegate to the existing shadow/cv/context/health code — no logic duplication. Registered at top level from src/cli/index.ts.

subItems.title: 'pan show <id>' default view is ≤ 25 lines and covers shadow, specialist, heartbeat, and cv
Each of --cv, --context, --health, --shadow scopes the output to that subsection
--json emits stable machine-readable output for scripting
Delegates to existing shadow/cv/context/health handlers (no copy-pasted logic)
Unit/integration test covers default view and each flag

narrative.Action: New src/cli/commands/review.ts. Subcommands: 'pending' (from work pending), 'request <id>' (from work request-review), 'reset <id>' with --session and --cycles flags (collapses work reset-review + work reset-session). Delegate to existing handlers.

subItems.title: 'pan review pending' matches 'pan work pending' output 1:1
'pan review request <id>' triggers the same re-review flow as 'pan work request-review'
'pan review reset <id>' resets review cycles; --session also clears Claude session
Integration test covers each subcommand and the --session flag

narrative.Action: New src/cli/commands/issues.ts. Merge 'pan work list' and 'pan work triage' into one top-level command. Use either --triage flag or 'issues triage' subcommand (decide during implementation based on ergonomic feel — document the choice in the bead's PR description).

subItems.title: 'pan issues' lists work across configured trackers (matches 'pan work list')
Triage mode is reachable via a single documented invocation (flag or subcommand)
Integration test covers both modes

narrative.Action: Promote 'pan work plan' and 'pan plan-finalize' into a top-level 'plan' command group. 'pan plan <id>' creates the plan (delegates to existing work-plan handler). 'pan plan finalize <id>' is the subcommand (delegates to plan-finalize.ts). Keep the legacy 'pan plan-finalize' path alive ONLY until this bead lands, then remove in the same commit.

'pan plan finalize <id>' finalizes identical to legacy 'pan plan-finalize <id>'
Legacy top-level 'pan plan-finalize' is removed in the same commit that adds 'plan finalize'

items.title: Update packages/contracts RPC types for new routes

narrative.Action: Update RPC type definitions in packages/contracts/ and the Effect RPC group in src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts to rename verbs: work/* procedures split into issues/*, review/*, show/*, admin/*. Rebuild packages/contracts ('cd packages/contracts && npm run build'). This is the type-propagation bead — it must land before routes and frontend client beads.

subItems.title: packages/contracts/ exports new procedure names matching the CLI taxonomy
Effect RPC group in ws-rpc.ts matches new names
'npm run typecheck' passes at workspace root after contracts rebuild

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/, rename all lifecycle verb routes (start/done/approve/close/reopen/wipe/tell/kill/resume/recover/sync-main/inspect) from /api/work/* to /api/issues/*. Router wiring only — handlers unchanged. Update the integration tests that hit these paths in the same commit.

subItems.title: All lifecycle routes respond at /api/issues/*
No handler code was duplicated — pure routing rename
Corresponding integration tests pass

narrative.Action: Rename review-loop routes (pending, request, reset) from /api/work/* to /api/review/*. Integration tests updated in the same commit.

subItems.title: Review routes respond at /api/review/{pending,request,reset}
Integration tests pass

narrative.Action: Rename the legacy shadow/cv/context/health endpoints from /api/work/* to /api/show/*. Integration tests updated in the same commit.

subItems.title: Show routes respond at /api/show/* with the same payloads as before
Integration tests pass

narrative.Action: Move plumbing routes (cloister, specialists, remote, db, beads, tracker) to /api/admin/*. Integration tests updated in the same commit.

subItems.title: Admin routes respond under /api/admin/*
All /api/work/* routes are fully removed from the router (verified by grep)
Integration tests pass

items.title: Update frontend RPC client and Zustand store

narrative.Action: Update WsTransport.ts, EventRouter.tsx, and the Zustand store in src/dashboard/frontend/src/ to call renamed routes and subscribe to renamed RPC events. Typecheck must pass after packages/contracts rebuild.

subItems.title: Frontend calls /api/issues/*, /api/review/*, /api/show/*, /api/admin/* — no /api/work/* hits
'npm run typecheck' passes in src/dashboard/frontend

narrative.Action: Grep src/dashboard/frontend/src/ for 'pan work', 'pan cloister', 'pan plan-finalize', 'pan specialists', etc. in shell hint components, copy-to-clipboard snippets, empty-state help text, onboarding flows, inspector panel. Replace with new verbs.

items.title: Update kanban card + inspector panel action labels

narrative.Action: Kanban card action buttons and inspector panel actions that call backend routes must show the new verb names in their UI copy and tooltips. E.g. 'Mark done', 'Approve & merge', 'Wipe' — with hover-tooltip showing the equivalent 'pan <verb> <id>' invocation.

subItems.title: Kanban action buttons use new verb labels
Inspector panel action labels and tooltips reference new verbs

narrative.Action: New component at src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/upgrade-announcement/. Renders the migration table from docs/QUICK-REFERENCE.md inline (embedded as a constant; do not fetch at runtime). Shows on first dashboard visit after upgrade. Dismissible. Persist dismissal in localStorage under a versioned key like 'panopticon:upgrade-banner:v0.x-taxonomy-reorg'. Renders at the top of the dashboard, above the kanban board.

subItems.title: Banner renders the full migration table from QUICK-REFERENCE.md on first visit
Dismiss button hides the banner and writes a dismissal key to localStorage
After dismissal, the banner does not reappear on page reload
Component has frontend unit tests covering render + dismiss

items.title: Update docs/USAGE.md and docs/INDEX.md for new taxonomy

narrative.Action: Rewrite docs/USAGE.md and docs/INDEX.md around the new command surface. Reference QUICK-REFERENCE.md for the migration table instead of duplicating it.

subItems.title: docs/USAGE.md uses only new verbs; no legacy paths
docs/INDEX.md lists pan admin and links to QUICK-REFERENCE.md

items.title: Update doc references in all PRDs and active plans

narrative.Action: Grep docs/ for legacy command invocations (pan work, pan cloister, pan specialists, pan plan-finalize, pan beads, pan db, pan remote, pan migrate-config, pan sync-costs, pan config shadow, pan setup hooks). Replace with new verbs in all doc files EXCEPT the PRD file itself (which describes the rename).

subItems.title: 'rg -n 'pan work |pan cloister |pan specialists |pan plan-finalize|pan beads |pan db |pan remote |pan migrate-config|pan sync-costs|pan config shadow|pan setup hooks' docs/ --glob '!docs/prds/planned/pan-command-taxonomy-reorg.md' --glob '!docs/QUICK-REFERENCE.md'' returns zero hits

items.title: Update hook scripts and installed shell aliases

narrative.Action: Update scripts/git-hooks/ and any Panopticon-installed shell aliases (shipped via 'pan sync') so they invoke the new verbs. Grep scripts/ for legacy invocations.

subItems.title: No legacy 'pan work' / 'pan cloister' / 'pan plan-finalize' invocations remain in scripts/

narrative.Action: Commit tests/fixtures/pan-help.txt with the full 'pan --help' output. Write a Vitest test tests/fixtures/pan-help.test.ts that spawns the CLI, captures --help, and asserts byte-for-byte equality against the fixture. Support UPDATE_FIXTURES=1 env var to rewrite the fixture in place (single update path). Establishes the repo convention for locking user-facing text surfaces — see STATE.md for rationale. DO NOT use Vitest '.snap' files.

subItems.title: tests/fixtures/pan-help.txt exists and matches current 'pan --help' byte-for-byte
Vitest test asserts equality; UPDATE_FIXTURES=1 rewrites the fixture
No '.snap' files added; test uses plain-text fixture only
Test file comment documents the UPDATE_FIXTURES workflow

items.title: pan doctor: flag legacy invocations in user shell rc

narrative.Action: Extend src/cli/commands/doctor.ts to grep the user's shell rc files (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, ~/.config/fish/config.fish) for 'pan work ', 'pan plan-finalize', 'pan cloister', 'pan specialists', 'pan sync-costs', etc. Print a warning with the replacement when found. Do not auto-rewrite — inform only.

subItems.title: 'pan doctor' warns on legacy invocations in shell rc files and shows the replacement
Doctor does NOT modify rc files — warning only
Test covers rc file scanning with fixture rc files

narrative.Action: Create .claude/skills/_template/pan-verb-skill.md — the shared template that the 8–10 flat shortcut skills extend. Template includes frontmatter slots for name/description, a standard body structure, and a pointer to the umbrella skill for users who want the taxonomy. 'pan sync' must NOT distribute _template/ to user skill dirs — extend the sync code to ignore paths starting with '_'.

subItems.title: .claude/skills/_template/pan-verb-skill.md exists with name/description placeholders and standard body
'pan sync' does not distribute files under .claude/skills/_template/
Template is referenced from STATE.md / CONTRIBUTING for future skill authors

narrative.Action: Create .claude/skills/pan/SKILL.md. Bare invocation: print the embedded six-bucket taxonomy cheat sheet (not fetched at runtime — inlined in the SKILL body). With args: instruct Claude to run 'pan <args>' as a single Bash invocation and report stdout/stderr. Destructive verbs (wipe, kill, close, approve, admin cloister emergency-stop) require chat-layer confirmation before the Bash call. Shell metacharacters in user args are refused with a message asking the user to run the command directly. Unknown top-level verb: print the cheat sheet and suggest the nearest match (prefix match, no Levenshtein required). Description field leads with 'pan <verb> <args> — Umbrella for all pan commands'.

subItems.title: '/pan' (bare) prints the embedded six-bucket taxonomy cheat sheet without any Bash tool invocation
'/pan start PAN-415' results in 'pan start PAN-415' being executed via the Bash tool and its output returned
'/pan admin cloister status' dispatches the nested subcommand correctly
Destructive verbs (wipe/kill/close/approve) echo the intended command and require confirmation before Bash call
Shell metacharacters (;, |, &&, $ (), backticks) in args are refused — skill instructs user to run directly
Unknown verb prints the cheat sheet plus nearest-match suggestion — no blind 'pan <gibberish>' invocation
Taxonomy cheat sheet is embedded in SKILL.md, not fetched from filesystem at runtime

items.title: Rename skills to match new CLI verbs 1:1

narrative.Action: Rename the distributed skills under .claude/skills/ so their directory names match the new CLI verbs. Drop pan-work-* prefix; collapse pan-plan + pan-plan-finalize into one pan-plan skill that takes a 'finalize' subcommand; move plumbing under pan-admin-* prefix (pan-cloister → pan-admin-cloister, pan-tldr → pan-admin-tldr, pan-rescue → pan-admin-rescue, etc.). Delete skills whose commands are being dropped (sync-costs, etc.). Update each skill's frontmatter 'name:' field to match the new directory name.

subItems.title: No skill directory is named pan-work-*
pan-plan-finalize directory no longer exists; pan-plan skill handles finalize subcommand
All plumbing skills are named pan-admin-* (cloister, tldr, rescue, beads, db, remote, specialists, etc.)
Each skill's frontmatter name: field matches its new directory name

narrative.Action: The flat top-level skills in the slash menu are exactly: pan, pan-status, pan-plan, pan-start, pan-show, pan-review, pan-done, pan-approve, pan-close, pan-issues. All other skills live under pan-admin-* and are surfaced only via the umbrella '/pan admin …'. The shortcut skills reuse the template from p55-verb-template for consistent maintenance. Each shortcut is tailored with verb-specific examples and flag guidance in its body.

subItems.title: Exactly these skill dirs are at the top level: pan, pan-status, pan-plan, pan-start, pan-show, pan-review, pan-done, pan-approve, pan-close, pan-issues
All other pan-* skills have the pan-admin-* prefix
Each shortcut skill extends the template and includes verb-specific examples

narrative.Action: Every distributed skill's frontmatter description MUST follow the format: 'pan <verb> <args> — one-line what it does'. The literal CLI invocation is always first so fuzzy search matches from both ends. Example: 'pan kill <id> — stop a running agent (workspace preserved)'.

subItems.title: Every skill frontmatter description begins with 'pan <verb>' followed by ' — ' and a short explanation
Lint/test script validates the description format for all pan-* skills

items.title: pan sync deletes legacy skill files on upgrade

narrative.Action: Update src/cli/commands/sync.ts and src/lib/sync.ts so that on next run, pan sync removes any legacy skill directories from ~/.claude/skills/ that are no longer in the distributed set. Use an explicit list of legacy names (pan-work-*, pan-plan-finalize, pan-rescue, pan-tldr, pan-cloister, pan-specialists, etc.) so removal is deterministic and auditable. Log what was deleted to stdout.

subItems.title: pan sync removes legacy skill directories from ~/.claude/skills/ on next run
Deletion uses an explicit allow/deny list (not 'anything not in current set'), logged to stdout
Unit test covers the cleanup path with a fixture skill dir containing legacy + new

items.title: Plain-text fixture test for synced skill set

narrative.Action: Commit tests/fixtures/synced-skills.txt listing the exact skill directory names that pan sync distributes (sorted, one per line). Write a Vitest test that enumerates the skill source dir and asserts equality against the fixture. UPDATE_FIXTURES=1 rewrites the fixture. Uses the same plain-text-fixture pattern as p5-snapshot-help. DO NOT use .snap files.

subItems.title: tests/fixtures/synced-skills.txt lists all distributed skill dir names sorted one per line
Vitest test asserts equality; UPDATE_FIXTURES=1 rewrites the fixture
Fixture contains no pan-work-*, pan-plan-finalize, or any legacy name

Entry embeds the full migration table (not just a link)

narrative.Action: At the very end of the work, move docs/prds/planned/pan-command-taxonomy-reorg.md → docs/prds/active/pan-705/pan-command-taxonomy-reorg.md. This signals the PRD has shipped. Update any incoming links (QUICK-REFERENCE.md uses a relative link to the planned path).

docs/QUICK-REFERENCE.md link to the PRD is updated to the active path
